_meta.note: All values for SG8 grade. Always round UP input to next table row (conservative). Spans in metres, sizes in mm.

topographic_class._note: All sites outside outer/crest zones are T1, except valleys with accelerated wind (T4) and gentle undulations <10m height (T1)

wind_zones._note: L=Low(32m/s), M=Medium(37m/s), H=High(44m/s), VH=Very High(50m/s), EH=Extra High(55m/s), SED=Specific Engineering Design

wind_bracing_demand_subfloor._lookup: H_apex_m → h_roof_above_eaves_m → {across, along} BU/m for High wind zone
wind_bracing_demand_subfloor._note: For other zones multiply by: L=0.5, M=0.7, VH=1.3, EH=1.6

wind_bracing_demand_single_upper._lookup: H_floor_to_apex_m → h_roof_above_eaves_m → {across, along} BU/m for High wind zone
wind_bracing_demand_single_upper._note: For other zones multiply by: L=0.5, M=0.7, VH=1.3, EH=1.6

wind_bracing_demand_lower_two._lookup: H_lower_floor_to_apex_m → h_roof_above_eaves_m → {across, along} BU/m for High wind zone
wind_bracing_demand_lower_two._note: For other zones multiply by: L=0.5, M=0.7, VH=1.3, EH=1.6

eq_bracing_single_subfloor._note: BU/m² for zone 3, soil D/E, 2kPa. Multiply by eq_multiplication_factors. Key: roof_wall_subfloor

eq_bracing_two_storey_subfloor._note: BU/m² for zone 3, soil D/E, 2kPa. Key: roof_upper_lower_subfloor

eq_bracing_slab._note: BU/m² for zone 3, soil D/E, 2kPa. Single and two-storey on slab. Key: roof_single/upper_lower

subfloor_bracing_capacity._note: BU capacity per bracing element type

pile_footings._note: 1.5 and 2 kPa floor loads. Min 350mm sq / 400mm dia for anchor/braced piles

bearers._note: Continuous over 2+ spans. Find smallest size where span >= required

floor_joists._note: Spans may increase 10% for continuous over 2+ spans

flooring_strip._note: Type A: radiata pine, matai, rimu, red/silver beech, Douglas fir, larch. Type B: tawa, hard beech, jarrah, karri, blackbutt, tallowwood, NZ hardwoods

studs_loadbearing._lookup: position → wind_zone → loaded_dim_m → height_m → spacing_mm → size
studs_loadbearing._note: Position: sot=single/top storey, lot=lower of two/subfloor beneath one, sub=subfloor beneath two

studs_no2_framing._note: Internal non-loadbearing walls, all wind zones

rafters._note: Spans for Extra High zone. Multiply by: L/M=1.3, H/VH=1.1. Fixing E=4.7kN, F=7.0kN

ridge_beams._note: Fixing types: G=4.7kN, H=8.5kN, I=16.0kN, J=24.0kN

ceiling_joists._note: Spans may increase 10% for continuous over 2+ spans. All wind zones.

underpurlins._note: Fixing L=9.8kN(2xM12), M=13.0kN(2xM16). Span +10% if continuous 2+ spans

fixing_types._note: Key to fixing types for rafters, trusses, underpurlins, ridge beams, strutting beams

slab_on_ground.anchor_bolts: M12 at 1200 crs, 150mm from plate ends

masonry_bracing._note: Bracing ratings for 2.4m high reinforced concrete or masonry wall elements. Walls must be >= 1.5m long.

top_plates._lookup: position → plate_size → [truss_position|floor_dim] → truss_spacing → roof_weight → stud_spacing → max_loaded_dim_m
top_plates._note: Values are max loaded dimension (m). null = not permitted. Column-major reading from PDF extract.

sot._note: (a) Single or top storey. Keys: truss_position (anywhere/within_150) → truss_spacing → roof_weight → stud_spacing → max_loaded_dim

lot._note: (b) Lower of 2 storeys and subfloor stud walls supporting 1 storey. Keys: plate_size → floor_dim → truss_spacing → roof_weight → stud_spacing → max_loaded_dim

bottom_plates._lookup: position → plate_size → [floor_dim] → joist_spacing → roof_weight → stud_spacing → max_loaded_dim_m
bottom_plates._note: Values are max loaded dimension of wall supporting roof (m). null = not permitted. Row-major reading for (a), column-major with H300/H400 correction for (b)/(c).

sot._note: (a) Single or top storey. Floor dim = NA. Joist spacings: 400, 450, 600mm.

lot._note: (b) One floor plus roof. Column-major reading with H300/H400 swap correction.

sub2._note: (c) Two floors plus roof. Column-major reading with H300/H400 swap correction.

top_plate_fixing._note: Fixing of top plate to studs/lintels at 600mm centres. Type A = 2/90x3.15 end nails (0.7kN). Type B = 2/90x3.15 end nails + 2 wire dogs (4.7kN).

nailing_schedule_s8._note: Nailing schedule for wall framing. Hand-driven and power-driven alternatives.

bottom_plate_external._note: Bottom plate to floor framing at external walls and internal bracing elements

bottom_plate_internal._note: Bottom plate to floor framing at internal walls (may be nailed to floor decking)

bottom_plate_trimmer._note: Bottom plate to trimmer not exceeding 4.2m long

sill_header_trimmer_2400._note: Sill/header trimmer to trimming stud, trimmer <= 2.4m

sill_header_trimmer_3000._note: Sill/header trimmer to trimming stud, trimmer <= 3.0m

sill_header_trimmer_3600._note: Sill/header trimmer to trimming stud, trimmer <= 3.6m

stud_to_plate._note_short_studs: For studs up to 2.7m, 2/90x3.15 power-driven end nails are sufficient

top_plate_140x35_to_90x45._note: Top plate 140x35 to 90x45 and top plate to lintel

trimming_studs_blocking._note: Trimming studs at openings, blocking, and studs at wall intersections

trimming_stud_to_doubled_stud._note: Trimming stud to doubled stud immediately under lintel

plywood_flooring._note: Structural plywood flooring for up to 2 kPa floor loads

underpurlin_struts._note: Members 90mm thick may be substituted with built-up members per 2.4.4.7

outriggers._note: All joints fixed with min 2/90x3.15 nails

fixing_types._note: Capacities are associated with fixing type, not fasteners. See individual selection tables for appropriate fixing type.

nailing_schedule_s6._lookup: joint → {hand: {nail, qty, location}, power: {nail, qty, location}}

nailing_schedule_s7._lookup: joint → {hand: {nail, qty, location}, power: {nail, qty, location}}

nailing_schedule_s10._lookup: joint → {hand: {nail, qty, location}, power: {nail, qty, location}}
nailing_schedule_s10._note: Many joints reference fixing types from other tables (10.1, 10.5, 10.7, 10.10, 10.11, 10.14, 10.15)

nailing_schedule_s13._lookup: joint → {hand: {nail, qty, location}, power: {nail, qty, location}}

jack_studs._lookup: storeys → bearer_span_m → stud_size → max_height for loaded_dim (m)
jack_studs._note: SG8, up to 2kPa floor load. No built-up member substitution allowed.

purlins_flat._note: Values show maximum permissible spacing per combo (most economical option). Closer spacings with lighter fixings also permitted. All fixing types determined for higher uplift loads at roof periphery.

purlins_edge._note: All fixing types determined for higher uplift loads at roof periphery.

driven_pile_spacing._lookup: floor_load → scenario → joist_span_m → {set25_m, set50_m, set100_m} (max pile spacing)
driven_pile_spacing._note: Set = set per blow of pile in mm. null means combination not permitted. Span is average of joist spans on either side of bearer.

strutting_beams._note: SED = Specific Engineering Design required. Fixing for continuous spans shall have double capacity. Members 90mm thick may use built-up per 2.4.4.7.

verandah_beams._note: Includes provision for rafters cantilevering max 750mm beyond beam. Continuous spans require double fixing capacity. Members 70/90mm thick may use built-up per 2.4.4.7.

cantilever_joists._note: SG8, up to 2kPa. 90/140 depth insufficient where cantilevered balustrades used. Load cases: light_Xm = light roof of X span, heavy_Xm = heavy roof of X span, 2kPa = 2kPa floor load only, balcony = balcony floor+balustrade only.

140x45._extraction_note: Some values may have column alignment issues from PDF extraction

190x45._extraction_note: Values may have column alignment issues from PDF extraction — verify against standard before use

240x45._extraction_note: Values may have column alignment issues from PDF extraction — verify against standard before use

290x45._extraction_note: Values may have column alignment issues from PDF extraction — verify against standard before use

_TODO_section_9._note: Tables 9.1 (post uplift) and 9.2 (post connections) need manual entry — Section 9 was not in the PDF extracts
